stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy holds talks in Azerbaijan, Saudi Arabia on ‘security and energy’
stories.summary: Ukraine ‘actively developing’ regional security arrangements for defence, food and energy, Zelenskyy says; Russia and Ukraine swap prisoners. What we know on day 1,522 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Azerbaijan on Friday for talks on “security and energy” , a senior Ukrainian official told AFP. Kyiv and Baku enjoy warm relations, with Azerbaijan repeatedly expressing support for Ukraine’s territorial integrity and sending humanitarian aid since the Russian invasion in 2022. Ti

stories.title: RAF Typhoons scrambled in response to Russian drone threat near Nato airspace
stories.summary: Jets flew from bases in Romania but did not open fire as potential targets stayed within Ukrainian airspace Two RAF Typhoons have been scrambled from a Romanian air base to engage Russian drones close to Nato airspace, although they did not open fire. British defence sources said the fighter jets did not enter Ukrainian airspace, contradicting reports that Russian drones had been shot down by the RAF there, an event which would have represented a major escalation in hostilities between the weste

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: Altman apologizes after OpenAI failed to alert police before fatal Canada shooting
stories.summary: OpenAI said the company had identified an account using abuse-detection efforts, but determined at the time it didn’t meet threshold for legal referral The head of OpenAI has written a letter apologizing that his company didn’t alert law enforcement about the online behavior of a person who shot and killed eight people in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia. In the letter posted on Friday, Sam Altman expressed his deepest condolences to the entire community. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hanged under the cover of war: letters and videos tell stories of Iran’s death row victims
stories.summary: Testimony emerges from Babak Alipour, who spent three years on death row before being taken to gallows in March Writing from his cell in the Rajai Shahr prison in the northern Iranian city of Karaj, Babak Alipour wanted to tell his friends about those who had already gone to their execution. There was Behrouz Ehsani, 69, the elder statesman of the group, who was “never angry” about their predicament. Then there was Mehdi Hassani, a 48-year-old father of three who he saw a couple of times in the

stories.title: Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse
stories.summary: Languorous tree dwellers from Guyana and Peru died from ‘cold stun’ in warehouse with no power or running water Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Wildlife officials in Florida said in a newly released report that dozens of sloths taken from South American rainforests for display at a controversial new tourist attraction in Orlando died in the care of their new owners. An incident report from the Florida fish and wildlife conservation commission (FWC) said that 31 of the mammals p

stories.title: US justice department steps in on behalf of xAI in Colorado regulation case
stories.summary: Move creates conflict between state and administration as Trump seeks federal framework over states handling issue Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US justice department said on Friday it had intervened in a lawsuit by Elon Musk’s xAI challenging a Colorado law aimed at regulating artificial intelligence systems. In its intervention, the justice department said the law violated the 14th amendment’s equal protection guarantee by requiring companies

stories.title: US millionaire big-game hunter dies after being crushed by elephants
stories.summary: Ernie Dosio, a 75-year-old vineyard owner, was hunting an antelope species in Africa when the incident occured Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox An American millionaire big-game hunter has died after being crushed by a group of elephants during a hunting expedition in Gabon. Ernie Dosio, a 75-year-old vineyard owner, was hunting yellow-backed duiker, an antelope species, in the central African country of Gabon when the incident occurred last Friday. Wh

stories.title: Colorado funeral home owner sentenced to 30 years in decomposing bodies case
stories.summary: Carie Hallford, 48, whose ex-husband, Jon, was earlier sentenced, expressed remorse over corpse abuse scheme Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The co-owner of a Colorado funeral home was sentenced in state court on Friday to 30 years in prison for her part in a corpse abuse scheme that involved hiding nearly 200 decomposing bodies. Carie Hallford, 48, was also sentenced to 18 years in prison earlier this month after pleading guilty to a federal fraud c

stories.title: Bod Péter Ákos: Egyértelmű a kormányváltás piaci megítélése, de maradtak kockázatok
stories.summary: Kitörő örömmel fogadták a befektetők a Tisza Párt elsöprő győzelmét, de a támogató hozzáállás megmaradása hosszabb távon attól függ, hogy modernizációs pályára áll-e Magyarország.

stories.title: Amikor a Hölgyek háromezresét elfoglalták a magyarok
stories.summary: Az Alpok egyik legkönnyebb 3000 méter magas hegyén meglepően sok magyar próbálkozik, viszont figyelni kell a csúcsrégióban, ha az időjárás zordra fordul.

stories.title: Orbán Anita miniszterelnök-helyettes is lesz a Tisza-kormányban
stories.summary: A külügyminiszteri pozíció mellett Orbán Anita a kormányüléseken és más hivatalos eseményeken is helyettesítheti Magyar Pétert.

stories.title: Ha nem teszünk semmit, Budapest éghajlata Észak-Iránéra fog hasonlítani
stories.summary: Ennek ellenére pálmafák nem fognak nőni a fővárosban, ugyanis a fagyok is velünk maradnak. De ami a legijesztőbb, hogy megugorhat az extrém időjárási események száma.

stories.title: A romák egy része úgy érezte, nem képviseli már őket a Fidesz, most megnézik, új reményt ad-e az új kormány
stories.summary: Kiábrándult volna a roma kisebbség a Fideszből és Orbán Viktorból? Vagy egyszerűen csak a nem mozgósított eléggé a Fidesz? Ennek járt utána a DW Magyar szerkesztősége.

stories.title: A MÁV azt írta a 444-nek, hogy ha adatokat akar, adjon be közadatigénylést – hogy aztán azt is lepattintsák
stories.summary: Különös köröket futtatott a lappal a Budapest–Belgrád-vasútvonal ügyében a MÁV.

stories.title: Felköszöntötte a Fővárosi Állatkert Samut, a kiselefántot
stories.summary: Samu idén lett ötéves, a veszprémi állatkertbe költözik majd.

stories.title: Szakértő: Az RMDSZ közvetlenül validálta a Fidesz propagandáját azáltal, hogy nem cáfolta
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt győzelme után Erdélyben is sokan tartottak attól, hogy Magyarország akár már másnap belép a háborúba, vagy megindul a sorozás, ami jó fokmérője annak, hogy milyen mélyen hatott a kampány során felépített háborús propaganda. A félelem nem spontán alakult ki: egy tudatosan felépített kommunikációs gépezet eredménye, amely éveken át torzította a valóságot és erősítette a fenyegetettség érzetét. Dr. Győrffy Gábor egyetemi docens szerint a Fidesz propagandája nem egyszerű politikai üzene

stories.title: Már nem egyszerűen lakói, hanem részvényesei vagyunk Magyarországnak
stories.summary: Az elmúlt hónapok egyik tanulsága: ha valaki hitelesen áll ki valami fontos dolog mellett, a közösség robbanásszerűen támogatni fogja.

stories.title: David Szalay: Eddig szégyelltem külföldön, hogy magyar vagyok, most már büszke vagyok rá
stories.summary: A Booker-díjas író örül a Tisza győzelmének, és abban bízik, hogy Magyar fel tudja majd számolni azt a korrupciót, ami Orbán bukását jelentette szerinte.

stories.title: Magyar Péter találkozott a VMSZ-szel, és azt mondta, nem szórhat Fidesz-propagandát a vajdasági tévé
stories.summary: Magyar jelezte a VMSZ elnökének, hogy alapvető változásokat várnak el az anyaországi források felhasználásának átláthatóságával kapcsolatban.

stories.title: Először csapódott lakott területre Romániában orosz drón, a román hatóságok több száz embert kitelepítettek, utcákat zártak le, felfüggesztették a gázszolgáltatást
stories.summary: Új szintre lépett a romániai drónincidensek sora: míg korábban jellemzően a Duna-deltában, ritkán lakott határ menti területeken találtak orosz drónmaradványokat, most először egy romániai város lakott övezetébe csapódott be egy, feltehetően robbanótöltetet is szállító drón. A Galac városában történt incidens miatt több száz embert kitelepítettek, lezártak egy egész környéket, felfüggesztették a gázszolgáltatást, Bukarest pedig bekérette Oroszország nagykövetét.

stories.title: „Magánrendelésen kellett odaadni a többszázezres vizsgálati díjat” – nem sikerült megtörni az orvosbárók hatalmát
stories.summary: A hálapénz kivezetése nem szabadította meg az egészségügyet a nagy hatalmú orvosok befolyásától, csak az eszközük változott: a magánellátást használták fel. A Magyar Orvosi Kamara szerint közvetve az orvosbárók a korszerűbb és igazságosabb egészségügy akadályai.

stories.title: Nem engedte ki élettársát egy férfi a kelenföldi lakásból, a TEK is kivonult a helyszínre
stories.summary: A férfit elfogta a rendőrség, a nő kijutott a lakásból.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strike on Odesa kills 2, hits hospitals and schools overnight
stories.summary: Ukraine said Russian forces struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa overnight on 23 to 24 April 2026, killing at least two people and injuring more than a dozen.

stories.title: Europe is in a profound state of crisis. Luckily, we know what to do | Nathalie Tocci and Anu Bradford
stories.summary: We assembled a group of the continent’s leading thinkers to assess the threats: their warnings are stark, but the remedy is within reach Caught between Vladimir Putin’s Russia, Donald Trump’s US and Xi Jinping’s China, Europe appears in a state of profound crisis, the narrative about its future often filled with fatalism. There is a paradox, however. Despite rising nationalism, the climate crisis and the economic slowdown , few would take issue with the claim that Europe still has a great deal g

stories.title: A Magyar Vakok és Gyengénlátók Országos Szövetsége is gratulált a leendő szociális és családügyi miniszternek
stories.summary: Dr. Kátai-Németh Vilmos kinevezése után a Hvg.hu-nak azt írták, örülnek, hogy 2026-tól a fogyatékossággal élő személyek a lehető legmagasabb szinten is képviselve lesznek.

stories.title: A lemaradók fejlesztése, a hibákból tanulás, a kreativitás felszabadítása érdekli a leendő oktatási minisztert
stories.summary: A tanárok azt gondolják, akkor dolgoznak meg a pénzükért, ha minél többet magyaráznak, de így a gyerekek alig jutnak szóhoz, mondta korábban Lannert Judit oktatáskutató, aki sokat foglalkozott a gyerekközpontú iskolával, a nyitott gondolkodásmóddal, a finn példa alapján pedig az elit oktatás helyett a tömegek felemelkedési lehetőségeivel.

stories.title: Bencsik András: A Fideszben idiótákra bízták a kommunikáció irányítását
stories.summary: A Demokrata főszerkesztője szerint próbálták kivédeni azt a nyomást a Fidesz részéről, hogy „valakik valamiért úgy gondolták, hogy a magyar dolgozó nép az olyan buta, mint egy pár rendőrcsizma”.

stories.title: „Egy jó pedagógus hagyja hibázni a gyerekeket” – gondolatok a leendő oktatási miniszter fél évvel ezelőtti interjújából
stories.summary: A magyar oktatás problémáiról és a lehetséges megoldásokról is beszélt Lannert Judit 2025. október 21-én, az Egyensúly Intézettel közösen készített Magyarország jövő időben című műsorunkban. Összegyűjtöttük a leendő oktatási miniszter legfontosabb gondolatait.

stories.title: Takács Péter szerint hiba volt, hogy nemzeti oldalként hivatkoztak magukra
stories.summary: A leköszönő egészségügyi államtitkár arról is beszélt a Patriótának, hogy üzenetértéke lett volna egy önálló egészségügyi minisztérium felállításának.

stories.title: Tárcákon átívelő vizsgálatot indítana a Magyar-kormány a koronavírus-járvány alatti beszerzések ügyében
stories.summary: Hegedűs Zsolt, a leendő kormány egészségügyi minisztere azt írta, a tervek szerint egy független szakértői vizsgálati mechanizmus is felállításra kerül.

stories.title: Különös, hogy az oroszoknak kémkedő, a börtön elől Moszkvába menekülő Kovács Béla nem volt Szijjártó radarján
stories.summary: Szijjártó Péter tagadta, hogy a Moszkvába menekült, és jelenleg is körözés alatt álló Kovács Béláról valaha is beszélt volna Szergej Lavrovval. Több körülmény azonban arra utal, hogy az Orbán-kormány egérutat adott az oroszoknak kémkedő egykori jobbikosnak.

stories.title: Rémutazás Vidnyánszky Attila koponyája körül
stories.summary: A Nemzeti Színház igazgatójánál jobban senkit nem foglalkoztatott az elmúlt években Marx életműve Magyarországon. Több évtizednyi készülődés után egy zűrzavaros, majdnem négy óra hosszú, befejezetlen agymenés lett belőle, aminek a választás fényében végképp semmi értelme. Kritika.

stories.title: Ebből maradt ki Orbán azzal, hogy kihagyta a lehetőséget élete talán utolsó EU-csúcsára
stories.summary: Versenyképesség, hétéves költségvetés, szolidaritás, Közel-Kelet, Ukrajna – bőven volt miről beszélni az uniós állam- és kormányfők informális találkozóján.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: Kushner, Witkoff to restart talks with Iran in Pakistan — without Vance
stories.summary: U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, the president’s son-in-law, will travel to Pakistan on Saturday to restart negotiations to end the war with Iran, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Friday. Vice President JD Vance, who joined Witkoff and Kushner in peace talks with Iranian officials earlier this month in Islamabad, will not be traveling to Pakistan but will be “on standby” to join the negotiations. “Steve and Jared will be heading to Pakistan tomorrow to hear

stories.title: Hegseth declares victory while preparing for more war with Iran
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth alternated between declaring victory in Iran while also pledging to inflict “maximum violence” on the country on Friday, continuing the Trump administration’s split-screen approach that juxtaposes a war that’s already won with continued troop buildups and demands for help from allies. The contradictory messaging — that the war is won but new fighting may be necessary — has been a hallmark of White House and Pentagon briefings since the opening days of the two-month

stories.title: ‘Shot across the bow’: How Washington plans to take on prediction markets
stories.summary: Washington’s scrutiny of the wild world of prediction markets is about to come to a head. The  landmark federal case  against a U.S. soldier who allegedly used confidential information to trade on the capture of then-Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro is ratcheting up calls for new guardrails around the fast-growing prediction markets. Lawmakers have voiced alarm that the alleged trades could have endangered a military operation with potentially fatal consequences. The charges came just

stories.title: White House insiders see Rubio on the rise as a potential 2028 pick
stories.summary: Some of President Donald Trump’s closest confidantes increasingly see Marco Rubio as a serious 2028 contender — an acknowledgement of the Floridian’s savvy handling of foreign conflicts and his ability to avoid political land mines. It’s a striking turnaround for a politician once dismissed as too hawkish, too establishment and  too scarred by a failed 2016 presidential campaign  to matter in a post-Trump GOP. But his work on deposing Venezuela’s Nicolas Maduro and his steadfast loyalt

stories.title: France and Greece call for delaying Covid debt repayment and for more EU bonds
stories.summary: ATHENS — French President Emmanuel Macron and Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis on Saturday both called for spreading out the repayment of the EU’s post-pandemic recovery plan and for more EU debt to finance the bloc’s investment priorities. “We got indebted during Covid. Today some tell us that we need to reimburse fast. It is idiotic,” Macron said during a press conference at the Maximos mansion in Athens alongside Mitsotakis. “Let’s extend that debt

stories.title: Top Democrats demand clarity from Jeanine Pirro over threat to reopen investigation into Jerome Powell – as it happened

Sign up for the Breaking News US email When Pete Hegseth was asked about Pope Leo XIV’s condemnation of the war in Iran , and comments from the US Conference of Catholic Bishops suggesting the conflict is not a “just war”, the defense secretary simply said that the pope was “going to do his thing” . “We know what our mission is,” Hegseth added. “We follow that the orders of the president. We’ve got lawyers all over the place looking at what we’re doing and why we’re

stories.title: Inside Chornobyl: 40 years after disaster, nuclear site still at risk in Russia’s war
stories.summary: In February 2025, a cheap Russian drone tore through Chornobyl’s confinement shelter. Workers warn the site of the world’s worst nuclear accident is not safe yet The dosimeter clipped to your chest ticks faster the moment you step off the designated path inside the Chornobyl nuclear power plant. Step back, and it slows again – an invisible line between clean ground and contamination. Above rises the “new safe confinement” (NSC) – the largest movable steel structure ever built, taller than the St

stories.summary: Former Liberal party senator, who was elected to state upper house in March, says flights ‘worth every cent’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation’s South Australian leader has paid back Australia’s richest person for private flights he took while campaigning in the state’s recent election. stories.title: Criminalisation of climate protesters in UK is counterproductive, research finds
stories.summary: Study of 1,300 campaigners finds arrests, fines and jail terms increase determination of activists to take direct action The criminalisation of direct action climate protests in the UK is counterproductive and increases the determination of activists to undertake disruptive demonstrations, according to a study of 1,300 campaigners. New findings suggest arrests, fines and lengthy prison sentences given to nonviolent climate protesters who have blocked roads or damaged buildings may actually radic

stories.title: Deadly Russian strikes hit Dnipro – video
stories.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five people in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said. It’s reported that at least 34 people have been injured in these strikes, which lasted 'practically all night' according to Dnipropetrovsk regional head Oleksandr Hanzha. The bodies of four people were found in the ruins of a house destroyed in overnight attacks, and workers continued to search for bodies on Saturday morning

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Palestinians vote in first elections since outbreak of war; Israel strikes Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Elections taking place in the occupied West Bank and also central area of Gaza We have some images coming through the newswires of Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi , speaking with Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, and other officials in Islamabad this morning. Araghchi arrived in Pakistan last night. He wrote on social media that his trip would focus on “bilateral matters and regional developments”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Colombia, Venezuela hold key talks in post-Maduro visit
stories.summary: Colombia and Venezuela announced military cooperation to combat border "mafias" during President Gustavo Petro's visit to Caracas. Petro is the first leader to travel to Venezuela after Nicolas Maduro's removal.

stories.title: Germany news: Ships to head to Med for possible Hormuz role
stories.summary: German Defense Minister Pistorius has said German naval ships will deploy to the Mediterranean. Berlin has pledged support in the Strait of Hormuz, post-war. DW has more.

stories.title: Australian women and children leave Syrian detention camp for Damascus – and potentially home
stories.summary: Repatriation attempt comes after group was turned around when leaving camp in February. Albanese government says it’s not assisting cohort Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren have left al-Roj camp in north-east Syria, seeking to return to Australia. The group is reportedly travelling across Syria by road to the capital Damascus, under the control of the Syrian government. Continue reading...

stories.title: Head of UK data watchdog voluntarily steps aside amid HR probe
stories.summary: LONDON — The head of the U.K.’s Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) has voluntarily stepped aside from official duties pending an HR investigation. In a LinkedIn post on Saturday , Information Commissioner John Edwards wrote: “I wanted to let you know that for the last few weeks I have voluntarily stepped aside from my duties at the ICO while an independent investigation into HR matters is undertaken.” “I am fully cooperating and engaged with the investigation and will report progress in due

stories.title: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
stories.summary: Small boat destroyed in video posted on social media as US campaign has killed at least 178 people since September The US military announced on Friday that it killed two people in an attack on a boat in the eastern Pacific, part of a series of deadly strikes on vessels in recent months which it claims are targeting “narco-trafficking” operations. The US Southern Command declared in a social media post on X that Gen Francis L Donovan directed Joint Task Force Southern Spear, the counter-narcotics

stories.title: Louisiana judge in abuse case belatedly recuses himself after ruling in favor of church on whose finance panel he sits
stories.summary: Kendrick Guidry, alone among judges, initially ruled that the state supreme court’s decision to uphold a ‘lookback window’ for abuse claims did not set a binding precedent Only one judge in Louisiana has ruled in favor of the Catholic church’s ongoing attempts to strike down a law there which allowed old abuse claims their day in court – even after a state supreme court decision upheld the constitutionality of that so-called “lookback window”. stories.title: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
stories.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

stories.title: King Charles to meet New York Mayor Mamdani during US visit
stories.summary: King Charles is expected to meet New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani in New York during his visit next week, according to two people familiar with the planning. The unlikely duo will both attend a wreath laying at the 9/11 memorial in downtown Manhattan on Wednesday. The event will also be attended by former New York Mayor Michael Bloomberg and other New York government officials, according to the two people, who were granted anonymity to discuss private plans. Mamdani, son of one of world’s most prom

stories.title: Iran’s foreign minister in Pakistan for peace talks
stories.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi touched down in Islamabad Friday night to negotiate a more lasting truce between Iran and the U.S. following a ceasefire agreement between the two countries. Araghchi met with Pakistani Army Chief Asim Munir and Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif in Pakistan’s capital on Saturday to discuss the situation in Iran, according to the prime minister’s X account. The foreign minister said that he would also also be traveling to Oman’s capit

stories.title: UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo
stories.summary: Internal email proposes US should reassess support for UK claim to islands because of lack of support for Iran war UK politics live – latest updates Downing Street has been forced to insist that Britain will not yield sovereignty over the Falkland Islands, after a leaked Pentagon email proposed the US should reassess its support for the UK’s claim on the islands because of a lack of British support over Iran. The memo reflected ways in which the Trump administration could punish Britain for fail

stories.title: Walking the dog and braving the paps: the art of the doorstep photo, from Keane to Mandelson
stories.summary: Former US ambassador and Labour peer joins a long line of people who have gone out to meet awaiting paparazzi head-on For a man at the centre of a storm that has rocked the political establishment, Peter Mandelson has spent the week looking remarkably relaxed. Day after day, as MPs have grilled civil servants over who knew what when about the former US ambassador’s security vetting, and police continue to investigate serious allegations over his own conduct, Mandelson has stepped out of his Rege

stories.title: ‘Cries of delight’ as Sumatran orangutan filmed using canopy bridge to cross road for first time
stories.summary: After a two-year wait, video of a young male crossing above a road gives hope that critically endangered species can survive habitat fragmentation The critically endangered Sumatran orangutan has been filmed for the first time using a canopy bridge to cross a road. In 2024, conservationists in the Pakpak Bharat district of North Sumatra in Indonesia built the bridge high over the Lagan-Pagindar road, which provides an essential route for local people but which became a barrier for animals. Conti

stories.title: How the Walsall rapist John Ashby exposed his misogyny rapping online
stories.summary: The 32-year-old jailed for life for a racially motivated sex attack on a Sikh woman had a collection of hate-filled uploads John Ashby is a man who did not hide his hatred of women. In fact, the rapist, who was sentenced this week to life in prison with a minimum of 14 years for a racially motivated sex attack on a Sikh woman, vented his misogyny online for all to see. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK schoolboys’ fatal hike remembered in Black Forest 90 years on
stories.summary: Relatives of London pupils and German villagers mark anniversary of ‘English misfortune’ that Nazis turned into propaganda coup On 17 April 1936, the bells of St Laurentius church in the Black Forest rang out to guide to safety a group of London schoolboys trapped in deep snow on a mountain hike gone very wrong. Ninety years on to the day, as the bells sounded again, there was hardly a dry eye in the congregation of British relatives and German villagers remembering the night that had brought to

stories.title: ‘Athens cannot operate as a giant hotel’: mayor vows to rescue capital from overtourism
stories.summary: Haris Doukas warns that with 700,000 residents and 8 million tourists, people are being pushed out of their neighbourhoods In the heart of ancient Athens, on narrow streets and around archaeological sites, visitor groups appear to be everywhere, snaking their way behind tour guides. Previously, officials would have welcomed such scenes. But for Haris Doukas, the socialist mayor who is determined to reclaim the capital’s congested city centre for its citizens, the start of tourist season leaves m

stories.title: ‘Astonishing’ discovery could help save children from deadly disfiguring condition
stories.summary: A previously unknown species of bacteria found in patients with noma could be key to creating treatments for the neglected tropical disease The “astonishing” discovery of a new bacteria could open the door to better ways to prevent, detect and treat a fatal and disfiguring childhood disease, researchers hope. Noma, which is fatal in 90% of cases without treatment , begins as a sore on the gums but goes on to destroy the tissues of the mouth and face. Continue reading...

stories.title: Two children die in Wolverhampton house fire
stories.summary: West Midlands police confirm deaths in blaze on Friday evening Two young children have died in a house fire in Wolverhampton, West Midlands police have confirmed. Emergency services were called to the property in the south of the city at about 8.30pm on Friday, with first responders attending from West Midlands police fire and ambulance services. Continue reading...

stories.title: Palermo ‘honoured’ by rumours Dua Lipa and Callum Turner might marry there in June
stories.summary: Italian newspapers claim singer and actor, who is tipped to be next James Bond, are planning ‘wedding of the year’ in the city Last July, Dua Lipa shared a series of photos on Instagram while on holiday in Palermo with Callum Turner, the British actor she had become engaged to weeks earlier. In these photos, the pair appeared radiantly in love with each other – and the Sicilian capital. There were pictures of the couple strolling through the city’s vibrant baroque alleys, admiring the ceiling fr

stories.title: Staff decry ‘constant turbulence’ under Trump’s labor secretary, as she blames ‘deep state’ in resignation
stories.summary: Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned amid allegations of an affair and steering grants to politically connected figures The secretary of the Department of Labor, Lori Chavez-DeRemer, resigned this week after several controversies surrounding her brief tenure at the helm of the agency. But labor officials say even though her troubled reign is over, the US labor authority remains in a state of “constant turbulence”. Chavez-DeRemer was under investigation over claims she had an affair with a subordinate an

stories.title: Partygate v Mandelson: Keir Starmer faces attack from his own playbook
stories.summary: Some familiar, arcane terms are returning to the fore as the Tories study the tactics Labour used against Boris Johnson The lexicon of a British parliamentary scandal is arcane. As Keir Starmer fights to remain prime minister, he has had to respond to a “humble address”, had his judgment picked over during an “emergency opposition day debate” and now faces the ignominy of a “privilege motion”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Syria arrests suspected leader of Tadamon massacre
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef is one of most-wanted fugitives in relation to slaughter of estimated 288 civilians under Assad A Syrian former regime official suspected of leading a notorious civilian massacre revealed by the Guardian – and who became one of the country’s most-wanted fugitives after the fall of Bashar al-Assad – has been arrested by security forces, Syria’s interior ministry announced. Amjad Youssef was captured in the Ghab plain area about 30 miles (50km) outside the city of Hama and had “been

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian strikes in Dnipro, blackouts in Karachi and Manchester City’s Erling Haaland – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: OpenAI apologizes for not reporting Canada mass shooter
stories.summary: British Colombia Premier David Eby said at the time that OpenAI had the opportunity to prevent the mass shooting.

stories.title: Palestinians vote in municipal elections in Gaza, West Bank
stories.summary: Palestinians are going to the polls in the occupied West Bank and a part of Gaza in a first vote since the Gaza war.

stories.title: India news: Raghav Chadha, 6 other AAP MPs join BJP
stories.summary: The exit of seven Aam Aadmi Party MPs has dented the party's strength in the upper house of parliament, sparking fears of political instability. DW has more.

stories.title: Japan: Raging wildfires cause mass evacuations
stories.summary: More than 1,000 firefighters are battling two blazes in northern Japan, with some 3,000 people urged to leave their homes. The fires come as the country's winters become increasingly dry.

stories.title: Mali: Explosions, gunfire rock main military camp near capital
stories.summary: Mali's army said unidentified "terrorist" groups had attacked several positions in the capital and elsewhere in the country.

stories.title: Tunisia suspends rights group amid widening repression
stories.summary: Tunisian authorities have ordered one of Africa's oldest rights groups to cease its activities for a month. The move comes as critics say the country is moving toward authoritarian rule.

stories.title: Zelenskyy ready to hold Ukraine-Russia talks in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country is ready to hold talks with Russia in Azerbaijan. Meanwhile, Russian attacks killed several people across Ukraine.

stories.title: US: Justice Department drops investigation into Fed Chair Powell
stories.summary: The closing of the investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell removes an obstacle to the appointment of Kevin Warsh, US President Donald Trump's pick for head of the central bank.

stories.title: Heated Rivalry, The Housemaid and Fourth Wing: TikTok launches BookTok bestseller list
stories.summary: The inaugural top 20 is entirely made up of female authors, with Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently.

stories.title: 31 sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
stories.summary: Many died due to conditions at a Florida warehouse where they'd been shipped, while others arrived from South America dead, authorities say.

stories.title: Iran’s FM Abbas Araghchi meets with Pakistan officials but rules out direct talks with US
stories.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are expected to arrive in Pakistan on Saturday ahead of a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran. Tehran, however, has ruled out direct talks.

stories.title: West Bank and part of Gaza vote in first municipal elections since war
stories.summary: Municipal elections in the West Bank and central Gaza mark the first vote since the Gaza war, with Fatah-linked lists dominant and Hamas absent from the race.

stories.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections are held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says he is ready to meet Putin in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: In a significant first visit to the South Caucasus since the start of Russia’s all-out war, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in a statement next to President Ilham Aliyev he is ready to meet Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan after the US-led diplomatic talks have stalled over the past weeks.

stories.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
stories.summary: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north of the country in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

stories.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

stories.title: Why investment in Caspian transit routes is crucial for energy security
stories.summary: As old energy routes buckle under the pressure of war and geopolitical disruption, governments and companies are converging on the Caspian region as the corridor that could keep Eurasia's energy flowing.

stories.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.title: US to allow firing squads, gas, and electrocution for federal executions
stories.summary: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

stories.summary: On Friday morning, souring relations between Europe and the United States reared its Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
stories.summary: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

stories.title: Europeans must recognize US, China and Russia are ‘dead against’ us, says Macron
stories.summary: Europeans have to step up and defend their own interests because the U.S., China and Russia are now all “dead against” them, French President Emmanuel Macron warned on Friday. “We should not underestimate that this is a unique moment where a U.S. president , a Russian president, a Chinese president are dead against the Europeans. So, this is the right moment for us to wake up,” Macron said in a discussion with Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis organized by the Kathimerini newspaper in Ath

stories.title: How frustration at Cop stalemates inspires first global talks on phasing out fossil fuels
stories.summary: ‘Coalition of the willing’ gathers in Colombia to try to bypass petrostate blockages of Cop summits and chart fresh path The world’s first Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels conference, co-hosted by Colombia and the Netherlands, takes place in Santa Marta, Colombia, from 24 to 29 April. A “coalition of the willing” – including 54 countries and various subnational governments, civil society groups and academics – will try to chart a new path to powering the world with low-carbon energy. Continu

stories.title: Tokyo workers encouraged to wear shorts to cut energy costs and keep cool
stories.summary: Officials hope more casual attire for public servants will save electricity during Iran war as summer heat approaches Public servants working for the Tokyo metropolitan government are being encouraged to swap their suits for shorts this summer to combat sweltering heat and rising energy costs caused by the US-Israel war on Iran . Inspired by Japan’s Cool Biz energy-saving initiative , Tokyo officials hope the measure will cut dependence on air conditioning. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pentagon email floats punishing NATO allies over Iran war
stories.summary: Washington has been considering sanctioning NATO allies over their non-involvement in the Iran war, with Spain in the spotlight after refusing the US use of its bases and airspace, according to reports.

stories.title: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
stories.summary: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas markets At the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel. China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage . Now they are turning their attention to what they are b

stories.title: Trump sending Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan to resume talks on Iran war
stories.summary: White House says its Middle East envoys will meet Tehran’s foreign minister in Islamabad Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump is sending his Middle East envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan to resume negotiations to end the war with Iran, which has lasted nearly eight weeks. The White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt confirmed the travel on Friday, saying that Witkoff and Kushner, Trump’s son-in-law, would meet Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, in Islamabad. Con

stories.title: Mother ends life at Swiss clinic four years after son’s death
stories.summary: Wendy Duffy died at Pegasos clinic in Basel as assisted dying bill in England and Wales fails to pass A grieving mother has ended her life at a clinic in Switzerland four years after the death of her only child. Wendy Duffy, 56, a physically healthy woman, died at the Pegasos clinic in Basel after struggling to cope with the death of her 23-year-old son, Marcus. Continue reading...

stories.title: US envoy and Trump’s son-in-law to travel to Pakistan amid hopes for renewed Iran peace talks – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. For the latest, read our full report: Trump sending Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan to resume talks on Iran war The EU’s foreign chief has said that talks with Iran should include nuclear experts otherwise “we will end up with a more dangerous Iran.” Speaking on Friday ahead of an informal summit of EU leaders in Cyprus, EU’s foreign chief Kaja Kallas said: “If the talks are only about the nuclear and there are no nuclear experts around the table, then we will end u

stories.title: Indians forced to return home as Iran war rocks Gulf economy
stories.summary: The Iran war has forced thousands of Indian workers to leave their jobs in Gulf nations and return home. Their future remains unclear, and now India itself is feeling the economic impact.

stories.title: How Iran is wielding the Strait of Hormuz as leverage
stories.summary: While the US is signaling a desire to negotiate, Iranians are playing for time — counting on the world's dependence on Persian Gulf energy exports and their strongest weapon, the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Lebanon should be part of an Iran peace deal, EU's Ursula von der Leyen says
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the past weeks had shown how "security is indivisible." She said a deal with Iran should include Lebanon and respect its "soverignty and territorial integrity."

stories.title: The silent power of memes
stories.summary: They are funny, provocative and sometimes tasteless. But memes are also powerful political tool, shaping our perception — usually without us noticing.

stories.title: Is the far-right AfD misusing its parliamentary questions?
stories.summary: The Alternative for Germany (AfD) has submitted thousands of official questions in state parliaments — a right of any elected party. But critics argue that their focus is meant to pressure political opponents.

stories.title: Ukraine: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
stories.summary: Russian President Putin "may or not may not" attend the G20 in Miami in December, says the Kremlin. Meanwhile, after securing an EU loan, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy continued his diplomatic offensive in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Iran war: Second attempt at peace talks takes shape
stories.summary: US officials Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are set to depart for Islamabad on Saturday with the intention of talks with Iran, the White House said. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has also landed in Pakistan.

stories.title: WHO approves first Malaria drug for babies
stories.summary: Children under five account for more than two-thirds of malaria deaths. A new drug offers hope for this vulnerable group.

stories.title: Powerful tornado tears across Oklahoma, damaging homes and blocking roads
stories.summary: A powerful tornado struck Oklahoma damaging homes, downing power lines, and blocking roads. The storm hit near Enid and surrounding areas, prompting emergency responses. No fatalities were reported, with only minor injuries confirmed so far.

stories.summary: Ukraine welcomed Prince Harry to Kyiv on Thursday 23 April 2026 for a two day visit that remained unannounced until his arrival.

stories.summary: Officials say the restrictions are for public safety, but businesses and the public are feeling the impact.

stories.title: Israeli police investigate after officers 'cut Palestinian flag' from skullcap
stories.summary: The British Israeli man says he was detained after someone took offence at his kippah embroidered with Israeli and Palestinian flags.

stories.summary: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: South Africa's top envoy to Ghana summoned over attacks on foreigners
stories.summary: A Ghanaian national was confronted by South Africans in a viral clip and questioned about his immigration status.

stories.title: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.title: Watch: 20,000 teddy bears highlight plight of missing Ukrainian children
stories.summary: Each bear in the installation in Washington, DC, is meant to represent one of the children Ukraine says have been abducted by Russia.

stories.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
stories.summary: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

stories.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
stories.summary: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.

stories.title: China car giant BYD says it can thrive without US
stories.summary: With the price of fuel rising China's BYD says it is positioning itself to benefit from the global shift away from fossil fuels.

stories.title: Italy tests R1 robotic museum guide in Turin’s historic Palazzo Madama
stories.summary: Italy is trialling the R1 robot guide at Turin’s Palazzo Madama to ease crowding and improve access, explaining centuries-old artworks to visitors.

stories.title: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.summary: Ha az iratmegsemmisítők beszerzéséből is kispórolták a pénzt, könnyebben összeállhatnak a ledarált NER darabkái – már ha van bármi értelme papírcafatokat gyűjtögetni. Iratmegsemmisítési körkép.

stories.summary: A leendő egészségügyi miniszter szerint az egyedi gyógyszerkérelmekről szóló döntések nem lehetnek fekete dobozban.

stories.title: Take action over officials in Kyrgyzstan ‘helping Russia evade sanctions’, MPs and peers say
stories.summary: British foreign secretary told to impose new measures as ruble-pegged cryptocurrency A7A5 is supported in country More than 20 MPs and peers have called on the foreign secretary to take action against institutions and individuals in Kyrgyzstan allegedly facilitating large-scale Russian sanctions evasion. They urged the UK to levy personal sanctions against three top Kyrgyz officials for their alleged role in facilitating Russian sanctions evasion more broadly, and more specifically for allowing

stories.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
stories.summary: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the

stories.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
stories.summary: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

stories.title: Spain responds to reported US plans to punish NATO allies
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly proposes a series of options to punish NATO allies for failing to provide support in the Iran war. Suspending Spain from the alliance was just one possibility it considered.

stories.title: France holds national tribute for soldier killed in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Minister for the Armed Forces, Catherine Vautrin, the Chief of Staff of the Army and General Pierre Schill preside over a national tribute ceremony in Montauban to honour the memory of Staff Sergeant Florian Montorio.

stories.title: Israel awaiting US green light to 'return Iran to Stone Age,' defence minister says
stories.summary: The war has spilled out of Iran and engulfed the region, leaving several thousand people dead, mainly in Iran and Lebanon, and continues to destabilise the global economy.

stories.title: UN rights chief alarmed by number of drugs-related executions in Singapore
stories.summary: Officials in Singapore maintain that the death penalty has made it one of the world's safest cities and a government survey in 2023 showed strong public support for capital punishment for serious crimes.

stories.title: Prince Harry tells Putin 'there is still a moment to stop this war', at Kyiv conference
stories.summary: Duke of Sussex was speaking at the Kyiv Security Forum during a surprise visit to show support for Ukraine after four years of war with Russia. The prince added that it was also 'a moment for America to show that it can honour its international treaty obligations'. His last visit to the country was in September 2025, when he discussed support for war veterans Continue reading...

stories.title: India voices anger after Trump shares comments calling it a ‘hellhole’
stories.summary: Foreign ministry calls remarks of rightwing podcast host shared by Trump ‘uninformed, inappropriate and in poor taste’ The Indian government has denounced a social media post shared by Donald Trump that described India as a “hellhole”, calling the comments inappropriate and “in poor taste”. On Wednesday, Trump posted a four-page transcription of remarks made by the conservative podcast host Michael Savage that denounced the US constitutional right to citizenship of everyone born in the country.

stories.title: Ice block stalls hundreds of Everest climbers at base camp
stories.summary: Officials assessing route after serac between base camp and camp one deemed unstable and too risky for climbers A large ice block on the route just above the Mount Everest base camp has forced hundreds of climbers and local guides to delay their attempt to scale the world’s highest peak. The serac between base camp and camp one was unstable and risky for climbers, said Himal Gautam of Nepal’s department of mountaineering on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Britain should seek to rejoin EU, says civil servant who led Brexit department
stories.summary: Philip Rycroft says promises on issues from economics to immigration have not lived up to expectations Britain should start talking about rejoining the EU, according to a former senior civil servant who ran the Brexit department. Philip Rycroft, who was permanent secretary of the Department for Exiting the EU, said the “argument was there to be won” about going back into Europe, adding that a “clear-headed appraisal of what is in the country’s best interests” was needed. However, he said rejoini

stories.title: Elképesztő rombolást, felperzselt földet hagy maga után Lázár János – interjúnk Vitézy Dáviddal, a következő közlekedési miniszterrel
stories.summary: Pár óra alatt már rengeteg szakember felhívta, aki visszajönne a területre dolgozni, mondta a Telex stúdiójában Vitézy, aki nem autósellenes, hanem tömegközlekedés-barát politikát szeretne folytatni.

stories.title: Milliárdokat szórt ki fű alatt a Fidesz-kormány a kampányarcainak és a holdudvarnak
stories.summary: Gyanús hátterű cégeknek és fideszes influenszereknek, például Pataky Attilának, Tóth Gabinak és Muri Enikőnek is jutott abból a közel 12 milliárd forintból, amit a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap még tavaly szétosztott, de egészen eddig megpróbált elrejteni.

stories.title: A Pedagógusok Szakszervezete üdvözli, hogy Lannert Judit vezetésével önálló minisztériuma lesz az oktatásnak
stories.summary: A szakszervezet szerint az új kormány ezzel stratégiai szintre emeli az ágazatot.

stories.title: 2027-től visszatér az F1-be a Török Nagydíj
stories.summary: 2031-ig lesz a versenynaptár része a török pálya.

stories.title: Semjén Zsolt beadta lemondását a KDNP elnöki pozíciójáról, de nem fogadták el
stories.summary: Semjén azt írta, a KDNP országos elnöksége egyhangúlag nem fogadta el a lemondását, de generációváltásra, személyi és politikai megújulásra van szükség.

stories.title: Rakéták helyett motorcsónakon érkező maszkos fegyveresek és helikopterről leereszkedő kommandósok
stories.summary: Trump előbb bombázással fenyegetett, ha Teherán nem hajlandó tárgyalni, majd mégis meghosszabbította a tűzszünetet. A Hormuzi-szorost lezáró Irán ellen bevezetett tengeri blokáddal akar célt érni. Mindkét oldal lefoglalt és megtámadott konténerszállítókkal és tankerekkel üzengetett a héten.

stories.title: Márki-Zay: Nálam kevésbé konfrontatívabb emberek is vannak, akik nagyobb egységet is tudnának teremteni
stories.summary: A hódmezővásárhelyi polgármester az ATV-ben reagált arra, hogy többen köztársasági elnököt csinálnának belőle, és arról is beszélt, hogy nyerhetett volna-e négy éve.

stories.title: Költségvetési csalás miatt adtak ki elfogatóparancsot a szegedi Fidesz elnöke ellen
stories.summary: Rádi Feríz korábban a DK-nak dolgozott agrárszakértőként, 2023-ban vette át a szegedi Fidesz vezetését.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely egy dolgot tud Ruff Bálint javára írni
stories.summary: Gulyás szerint Ruff akkor is balliberális volt, amikor több mint húsz évvel ezelőtt a korosztályukban majdnem mindenki fideszes volt, és ebből a szempontból nem változott.

stories.title: Krausz Ferenc alapítványa: Nem leszünk ATM
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas tudós szerint a Nemzeti Tudósképző Akadémia, aminek a támogatásából márciusban kihátrált, nem átláthatóan és mérhetően működik, és a kért adatokat sem szolgáltatta nekik.

stories.title: Két évig bántalmazta a feleségét, aki aztán leugrott egy felüljáróról. A férfi elleni ítélet mérföldkő a skót igazságszolgáltatásban
stories.summary: Kimberly Milne 2023-ban vetett véget életének. A skót rendőrség az ügy mélyére ásott, április közepén pedig a nő bántalmazó férje 8 év börtönbüntetést kapott. Ez az első eset Skóciában, hogy valakit büntetőjogilag felelősségre vontak a partnere öngyilkosságáért.

stories.title: Trumpék visszahoznák a kivégzőosztagokat a halálbüntetések végrehajtásánál
stories.summary: Jelenleg öt államban van erre lehetőség, de kiterjesztenék ennek lehetőségét szövetségi ügyekre is.

stories.title: Kirúgtak egy ukrán parancsnokot, miután súlyosan alultáplált katonákról kerültek ki képek
stories.summary: A fotókon négy férfi szerepel kilátszó bordákkal, vékony karokkal.

stories.title: Germany unveils first-ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
stories.summary: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

stories.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
stories.summary: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

stories.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

stories.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
stories.summary: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

stories.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind
stories.summary: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

stories.title: US and Iran continue seizing ships in tense Hormuz standoff
stories.summary: The US and Iran continued seizing ships in the tense Hormuz standoff, with Tehran calling Washington's blockade a “ceasefire violation” and claiming it has been collecting transit fees for passage.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV ends Africa tour with open-air mass in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV held a mass in Malabo, marking the end of his 11-day Africa tour.

stories.title: US still delivering weapons to Ukraine despite Iran war, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Meanwhile, three people were killed and 10 others wounded in a Russian drone attack on the central city of Dnipro.

stories.title: UN 'working on' keeping presence in Lebanon after UNIFIL mandate expires in 2027
stories.summary: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon has served fulfilled peacekeeping duties between Israel and Lebanon since 1978 but finds itself caught in the crossfire between Israeli forces and Hezbollah.

stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended by three weeks, Trump says
stories.summary: Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, which was set to expire on Monday, by three weeks. Meanwhile, the US and Iran persist in its tense standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war sparks push to transform Syria into global energy corridor
stories.summary: Washington is reportedly exploring a plan to turn Syria into a key energy corridor, linking their pipelines to Turkey and Europe.

stories.title: Over 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN Women report estimates
stories.summary: From becoming the head of a household and facing challenges linked to housing and land rights, to restricted access to essential healthcare and reproductive services, how are women disproportionately affected by war?

stories.title: Armenia marks 111th anniversary of 1915 massacres in Yerevan
stories.summary: Armenia marked the 111th anniversary of the 1915 massacres on 23 and 24 April 2026 with marches and ceremonies in Yerevan, drawing thousands of participants.

stories.title: 20,000 teddy bears in Washington highlight abducted Ukrainian children
stories.summary: A sea of 20,000 teddy bears on the US National Mall highlights Ukrainian children abducted since 2021, urging action as the war’s toll on families grows.

stories.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
stories.summary: The widely circulated image had prompted authorities to move their search operation.

stories.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English
stories.summary: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.

stories.title: 'A dangerous standoff' as Strait of Hormuz blockade continues
stories.summary: The US and Iran's rival blockades of the Strait of Hormuz have become a "test of wills", says Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: Iran 'trapped in slogans' as nuclear talks with US stall after naval standoff, former diplomat says
stories.summary: Former Iranian diplomat Kourosh Ahmadi has warned that Tehran and Washington have found themselves stuck in their individual and contrasting narratives, risking nuclear talks as tensions over blockades and enrichment grow.

stories.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.title: Ukraine’s Chernobyl ‘liquidators’ return 40 years after disaster
stories.summary: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the 1986 Chernobyl disaster in April 2026 as former clean-up workers returned to the site.

stories.title: US soldier charged with using Maduro raid intel to win €341,000 on Polymarket
stories.summary: Polymarket said in a statement it had flagged the user who made the bets to the US Department of Justice and cooperated with the investigation.

stories.title: Poisoning suspected in deaths of 18 wolves in Italian national park
stories.summary: After the carcasses of 10 wolves were found last week, another eight have been found in recent days.

stories.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.title: Indonesian peacekeeper dies of wounds suffered in Lebanon last month, UNIFIL says
stories.summary: His death brings the number of peacekeepers killed since the start of the most recent war between Israel and Hezbollah on 2 March to six.

stories.title: The international right has CPAC. Has the left finally found its answer? | Owen Jones
stories.summary: Spain’s PM, Pedro Sánchez, hosted the inaugural meeting of the Global Progressive Mobilisation. Keir Starmer and other social democrats were notable by their absence Can progressives push back the rising tide of authoritarianism? Thousands of people gathered in Barcelona this weekend in search of an answer. The occasion was the inaugural Global Progressive Mobilisation – an ambitious initiative backed by the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez – which drew an impressive cast: Brazil’s Lula, Me

stories.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder i

stories.title: Opinion: The courage to speak
stories.summary: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, my experience as a rabbi showed that even small moments of dialogue can begin to rebuild fragile bridges between communities.
